title: Imperial IDE operational station UX hardening, real agent roster, taskpack manager, viewers, dirty classifier, and live registration promotion
mission_summary: Harden the Operational Station MVP into a daily-usable operator interface: human summaries, full JSON view, copy/open actions, real 12-servitor roster, taskpack manager, launch/handoff card viewers, reports/receipts browser, dirty classifier, safety center 2.0, lifecycle UI, git closure UI, and live-registration promotion flow.

strategic_intent.owner_goal: The Owner must operate the system from inside Imperial IDE without manually hunting paths, JSON fragments, or ZIP files.
strategic_intent.kernel: Imperium New Reality remains the script-first AI MetaOS kernel.
strategic_intent.station: Operational Station becomes the practical daily workflow layer.
strategic_intent.tui: Workbench TUI is the fast operator console and must retain the liked visual style.
strategic_intent.gui: Workbench GUI is the visual cockpit and must show the same truthful station state.
strategic_intent.agents: All agents are visible as organ servitors. Servitor Prime is the external LLM handoff servitor until live backend is opened.
strategic_intent.truth: The station must distinguish dry-run, candidate, canon, live, blocked, warning, failed, and completed states.
strategic_intent.safety: Real servitor execution, live LLM backend, arbitrary shell, and remote contours remain gated.

organ_route.DOCTRINARIUM: operational doctrine, agent taxonomy, state vocabulary, no-overclaim rules
organ_route.OFFICIO_AGENTIS: owner-facing summaries, Russian runbooks, labels, launch card copy, human UX wording
organ_route.ASTRONOMICON: dry-run to live local promotion, registration receipts, current expected task visibility, launch card state
organ_route.ADMINISTRATUM: reports browser, receipts browser, dirty classifier ledger, continuity, taskpack archive, git closure receipts
organ_route.MECHANICUS: tool and command validation, taskpack validation, safety checks, unknown-tool blocks, JSON viewer safety
organ_route.INQUISITION: fake-green blocking, safety center 2.0, candidate versus canon truth, dirty risk classes
organ_route.STRATEGIUM: operator workflow, menus, taskpack manager UX, next-step recommendations, daily-use KPD
organ_route.SCHOLA_IMPERIALIS: training mode, user guide, recipes, smoke workflow lessons

cyrillic_in_taskpack.scope: MANIFEST.json, TASK_SPEC.md, ACCEPTANCE_GATES.md, OUTPUT_REQUIREMENTS.md, TASK_ROUTE_MANIFEST_TEMPLATE.json, TASK_START_ACK_TEMPLATE.json, BLUEPRINTS/*.md

localization_exception.scope: owner-facing runtime files only after Officio role entry
localization_exception.forbidden_scope: taskpack internal root files and BLUEPRINTS

allowed_when: created or modified Python files compile
Workbench TUI opens or exact blocker is recorded
Workbench GUI structural smoke passes or exact blocker is recorded
taskpack manager can list inspect validate and expose generated TASKPACK.zip paths
reports and receipts browser can show summaries and raw paths
dirty classifier identifies old unrelated ZIPs fresh generated artifacts runtime files secrets and stage candidates
live registration promotion requires owner confirmation and does not auto-replace active current task
forbidden_push_cases: failed validation without explicit BLOCK verdict
destructive cleanup or delete without owner batch approval
live LLM backend enabled with secrets
legacy Alpha/Beta/Gamma remains the only agent display
UI claims execution done when only handoff is ready
dry-run registration is displayed as live canon
candidate task is displayed as canonical current task

primary_outputs: real 12-servitor agent roster as primary view
full JSON viewer and human summary renderer
copy/open actions for files folders ZIPs receipts launch card handoff card
Launch Card and Handoff Card viewer
Git Closure UI with dirty classification
documentation receipts validation commit and push
